_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_PLAYER: === CLASS PROGRESSION IMPORTER ===
This template lets you add ANY D&D 5e class to the level-up wizard.
The app has built-in data for Fighter, Rogue, and Bard (levels 2-10).
Use this to add: Wizard, Cleric, Paladin, Ranger, Sorcerer, Warlock,
Monk, Druid, Barbarian, Artificer — or extend existing classes to level 20.
HOW TO USE:
1. Open Gemini (gemini.google.com) or any AI chat
2. Paste this entire file
3. Tell the AI what class you need:
'Generate full Wizard progression levels 2-20 with School of Evocation
and School of Divination subclasses'
OR
'Generate Paladin progression levels 2-20 with Oath of Devotion
and Oath of Vengeance subclasses'
4. Copy the JSON output
5. In the app: tap ☰ hamburger menu > '📖 Import Class Progression'
6. Paste the JSON and tap Import
7. Now when any character of that class levels up, the wizard will offer
the correct features, subclass choices, spell selections, and ASIs
TIPS:
- You can import multiple classes at once — just include them all in the same JSON
- Imported data merges with built-in data (doesn't replace it)
- If you import a class that already has built-in data (e.g. Fighter),
imported levels override the built-in ones for those specific levels
- Import syncs to all devices via Firebase — only need to do it once
- You can re-import to add more subclasses or fix mistakes
_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_AI: INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE AI:
Generate D&D 5e class progression data for the level-up wizard.
Use the EXACT JSON structure shown below. The output is consumed by the app's
level-up wizard which presents steps: HP roll → auto features → choices → confirm.
TOP-LEVEL STRUCTURE:
{ "classname": { hit_die, slots, spellList, levels } }
- classname: lowercase class name (e.g. 'wizard', 'paladin')
- hit_die: number (6, 8, 10, or 12)
- slots: spell slot progression keyed by character level (omit for non-casters)
- spellList: spell names organized by spell level (omit if using app's built-in spell database)
- levels: object keyed by level number (2-20), each with 'auto' and 'choose' arrays
SLOTS FORMAT (for casters):
Key = character level, Value = array of max slots per spell level
Index 0 = 1st level slots, index 1 = 2nd level, etc.
Include entries for every level where slots change.
Half-casters (Paladin/Ranger): start at level 2 with [2]
Full casters (Wizard/Cleric/etc.): start at level 1 with [2]
Warlock: use pact magic slots (all same level), e.g. level 5: [2] (2 3rd-level slots)
SPELL LIST FORMAT (optional — only include if the class isn't in the app's spell database):
Key = spell level (0 = cantrips), Value = array of spell names
Include all spells the class can choose from at each level
LEVELS FORMAT:
Each level has 'auto' (automatic features) and 'choose' (player choices).
auto: array of strings — features gained automatically at this level.
Each string should be: 'FEATURE NAME: Description.'
For subclass-specific features, prefix with subclass name:
'School of Evocation: Sculpt Spells — create safe pockets for allies in your AoE spells.'
Include ALL features for ALL subclasses in the auto array — the app displays all of them.
choose: array of choice objects. Valid types:
SUBCLASS CHOICE (level 1-3 depending on class):
{ "type": "subclass", "prompt": "Choose Arcane Tradition", "options": ["School of Evocation", "School of Divination"] }
ASI / FEAT CHOICE (levels 4, 8, 12, 16, 19 — Fighter gets extras at 6, 14):
{ "type": "asi", "prompt": "Ability Score Improvement" }
SPELL CHOICE (for classes that learn/prepare new spells on level-up):
{ "type": "spell", "prompt": "Choose 1 new Wizard spell (up to 3rd level)", "count": 1, "tier": 3 }
- count: how many spells to pick
- tier: max spell level available
- cantrip: true if cantrips are also available in this pick (optional)
RULES FOR EACH CLASS:
- Include EVERY level from 2 to 20 (level 1 is character creation, not level-up)
- Include subclass choice at the correct level (Wizard 2, most classes 3, Cleric 1)
- Include ASI at correct levels (most: 4,8,12,16,19; Fighter: 4,6,8,12,14,16,19; Rogue: 4,8,10,12,16,19)
- For prepared casters (Cleric, Druid, Paladin): don't include spell choices in 'choose' —
they prepare from their full list each day, not on level-up
- For known casters (Bard, Sorcerer, Ranger, Warlock): include spell choices on levels where
they gain new spells known
- Wizards: include spell choices (they add 2 spells to their spellbook per level)
- Include proficiency bonus changes in auto features when they change (levels 5, 9, 13, 17)
- Include scaling features (Sneak Attack dice, Bardic Inspiration die, etc.)
OUTPUT: Return ONLY the JSON object with class data. No markdown code fences, no commentary.
Example: { "wizard": { "hit_die": 6, "slots": {...}, "levels": {...} } }

choose.prompt: Add 2 spells to your spellbook (up to 1st level)

auto: School of Evocation: Sculpt Spells — When you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures, choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. Those creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell and take no damage.

School of Divination: Portent — After finishing a long rest, roll 2d20 and record the numbers. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature you can see with one of these foretelling rolls.

choose.prompt: Add 2 spells to your spellbook (up to 2nd level)
Add 2 spells to your spellbook (up to 2nd level)
Add 2 spells to your spellbook (up to 3rd level)
